prompt: I want to create a general Korean resume that I can use for multiple applications to Korean companies. I'm mid-career with 5 years of full-stack experience. Use my CV from resumes/ and make it look like the Jumpit reference examples. 2-3 pages, clean layout.

assertions.name: references_jumpit_style
assertions.description: Response references the Jumpit layout examples or the experienced developer pattern (page 1: header, About Me, Skill Set, Work Experience; later pages: Projects)

assertions.name: cv_source_identification
assertions.description: Response mentions which CV file from resumes/ would be used or describes the process of selecting the best-matching CV

assertions.name: multi_page_structure
assertions.description: Response indicates 2-4 page structure, NOT one-page compression, and describes how projects would be organized across pages

assertions.name: korean_content_guidance
assertions.description: Response discusses translation approach (concise bullets, factual statements) or mentions keeping Korean content honest/verifiable

assertions.name: output_path_clarity
assertions.description: Response suggests output path (e.g., output/korean-resume/ or output/general-korean/) for a general-use resume

assertions.name: verification_steps
assertions.description: Response mentions visual review steps (page count, Korean glyph rendering, table alignment, page breaks) for quality assurance
